terms.label: Natural language mode
terms.description: Natural language mode turns a plain-language experiment request into validated Circuit IR before it is simulated. The generated structure is checked before any diagram, result, or code export is shown.

terms.label: JSON and YAML manifest
terms.description: JSON and YAML mode accepts a precise, portable circuit manifest. Algorithm Studio validates its qubits, operations, parameters, and backend settings before visualization or local simulation.

terms.label: Sample prompts
terms.description: Sample prompts are tested starting points for Bell pairs, GHZ states, rotations, Grover search, and quantum random numbers. Selecting one fills the experiment request without bypassing validation.

terms.label: Simulation engine
terms.description: The engine selector chooses the local simulation adapter. The interface can generate equivalent circuit representations while keeping results simulation-only; it never submits a paid quantum hardware job.

terms.label: Templates
terms.description: Templates generate known educational circuits such as GHZ, Grover, Deutsch-Jozsa, and quantum random number generation. They provide deterministic lesson starting points through the same validation path.

terms.label: Run
terms.description: Run validates the experiment, builds Circuit IR, executes the selected local simulator, and refreshes the circuit, state, measurement, and code panels from one shared result.

terms.label: Circuit IR
terms.description: Circuit IR is Algorithm Studio's validated source of truth. The circuit diagram, simulator, step inspector, measurements, state amplitudes, and generated Qiskit and Cirq code all consume that same structure.

terms.label: Step controls
terms.description: The step controls move before the first operation, backward or forward one operation, or after the final operation. This lets a learner compare the state immediately before and after each gate.

terms.label: Bloch sphere
terms.description: The Bloch sphere visualizes the selected qubit's local state using X, Y, and Z coordinates. An entangled qubit may not have a complete individual state arrow because its physical meaning belongs to the joint multi-qubit state.

terms.label: Measurement probabilities
terms.description: Measurement probabilities come from the selected local simulator and show how frequently each classical bit string would be sampled. Bit strings use the documented right-to-left Qiskit convention.

terms.label: State amplitudes
terms.description: State amplitudes show the magnitude and phase of each computational basis state before measurement. Squaring an amplitude's magnitude gives that basis state's measurement probability.

terms.label: Code exports
terms.description: Qiskit and Cirq code are generated from the same validated Circuit IR used by the diagram and simulator. The exports therefore represent the circuit currently being inspected rather than a separate model response.

terms.label: Hadamard gate
terms.description: The Hadamard gate creates or removes an equal superposition. Applied to zero it produces equal amplitudes for zero and one, with relative phase determining how a later Hadamard interferes.

terms.label: Pauli X gate
terms.description: The Pauli X gate is the quantum analogue of a classical bit flip. It exchanges the zero and one computational basis states while preserving quantum coherence.

terms.label: Controlled NOT gate
terms.description: The controlled NOT gate flips its target only when its control is one. When the control is in superposition, that conditional behavior can create entanglement between the two qubits.

terms.label: R Y rotation
terms.description: The R Y gate rotates a qubit around the Bloch sphere's Y axis. Its angle controls how amplitude and measurement probability move between the zero and one states.

terms.label: SWAP gate
terms.description: The SWAP gate exchanges the quantum states held by two qubits without measuring them. It is represented as a reversible three-CNOT operation in common gate decompositions.

terms.label: Measurement gate
terms.description: Measurement converts quantum information into a classical outcome sampled according to state probabilities. It ends coherent evolution for the measured information in the circuit model.

terms.label: Grover search
terms.description: Grover search begins with an equal superposition, marks a target with a phase oracle, and applies diffusion to amplify that target's amplitude. The two-qubit lesson searches four possibilities for the marked state.

learn.title: Learn

play_lesson.confirmation: I toggled the narration for the lesson currently on screen.

set_learning_level.confirmation: I changed the learning depth shown on screen.

open_lesson.confirmation: I opened the registered lesson on screen.

learn.description: This is the Learn workspace. Choose a learning level, move through the course and lesson tabs, listen to narration, inspect interactive quantum visuals, and answer checkpoints that test predictions rather than memorization.

circuits.title: Algorithm Studio
circuits.description: This is Algorithm Studio. Describe or select an experiment, run the validated local simulation, inspect Circuit IR, step through the gates, and compare the state, measurements, and generated code.

improve.title: Circuit Improvement Lab

run_review.confirmation: I started the bounded review using the objective and iteration limit shown on screen.

schedule_review.confirmation: I requested the scheduled review using the date and settings shown on screen.

improve.description: This is the Circuit Improvement Lab. Set a review objective, choose whether to run now or schedule a review, and compare candidates only when simulated statevector equivalence passes and the circuit score improves.

use-cases.title: Quantum Use Case Center

open_related_experiment.confirmation: I opened the registered educational experiment related to this use case.

use-cases.description: This is the Quantum Use Case Center. Compare a real decision problem with its strongest classical baseline, resource assumptions, current hardware limits, claim strength, primary evidence, and explicit exit criteria before funding an experiment.

drug.title: Drug Discovery Lab

run_drug_study.confirmation: I ran the educational simulated study with the molecule and settings shown on screen.

drug.description: This educational drug-discovery lab demonstrates how a molecular question can be translated into a small simulated quantum workflow. It does not predict clinical outcomes or replace chemistry validation.

providers.title: Provider Lab

run_annealer.confirmation: I ran the current QUBO with the local educational annealer.

providers.description: This provider workspace compares computing paradigms and access constraints without treating a vendor claim as verified performance. Use it to identify required evidence and a suitable independent evaluation.

benchmarking.title: Benchmark Intelligence

open_qpu_match.confirmation: I opened QPU Match. It compares bundled historical evidence and is not procurement guidance.

benchmarking.description: This benchmark workspace compares normalized public evidence. Different benchmark families are not interchangeable, missing evidence means unknown, and exploratory forecasts are not hardware or procurement guarantees.

podcast.title: Quantum Podcast

play_podcast.confirmation: I toggled playback for the episode currently selected on screen.

podcast.description: This podcast workspace provides chaptered audio lessons with transcripts. Use the episode list, playback speed, chapters, and transcript together to review a concept at your own pace.

community.title: Community

focus_community_form.confirmation: I moved to the inquiry type. You remain in control of all personal information and submission.

community.description: This community workspace accepts research, contributor, and reviewer inquiries. Review the consent and retention note before submitting personal details.

faq.title: 1StopQuantum FAQ
faq.description: This is the 1StopQuantum FAQ workspace. Browse concise saved answers about local simulation, prerequisites, learning order, AI-assisted accuracy, privacy, measurement variation, and reporting confusing explanations.

guide.title: Documentation
guide.description: This is the 1StopQuantum documentation workspace. Search getting-started guidance, quantum fundamentals, workflow instructions, local-first architecture, command references, and troubleshooting.
